{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tsukamyojin Kofun]][[en:draft:Tsukamyojin Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tsukamyojin Kofun|Tsukamyojin Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takatori, Nara]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tsukamyojin Kofun]][[en:draft:Tsukamyojin Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tsukamyojin Kofun|Tsukamyojin Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further
