Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tsukamyojin Kofun]][[en:draft:Tsukamyojin Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tsukamyojin Kofun|Tsukamyojin Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, scale, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its scale, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tsukamyojin Kofun]][[en:draft:Tsukamyojin Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tsukamyojin Kofun|Tsukamyojin Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal shape with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. Artifacts such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. Artifacts recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designated_as = Not designated }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designated as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designated as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designation1_as = Not designation1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designation1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designation1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designation1_as = Not designation1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designation1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designation1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designation1_as = Not designation1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designation1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designation1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | form = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | designation1_as = Not designation1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not designation1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden platform. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially designation1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:Ancient tombs in The tomb is not other_designation1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | other_designation1_as = Not other_designation1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not other_designation1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially other_designation1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | other_designation1_as = Not other_designation1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not other_designation1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially other_designation1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | other_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社}}) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 20.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 47 | long_sec = 10.88 | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} | other_designation1_as = Not other_designation1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not other_designation1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially other_designation1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:Ancient tombs in The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
