Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 20.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 10.88 | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} {{draft Kofun | alternate_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 20.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 10.88 | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} {{draft Kofun | alternate_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 20.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 10.88 | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 20.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 10.88 | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} {{draft Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 20.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 10.88 | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} {{draft But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tsukamyojin Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tsukamyojin Kofun|Tsukamyojin Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukamyojin Kofun (束明神古墳) | image = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture (within the grounds of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Takatori, Nara)|ja|春日神社|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 20.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 10.88 | type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diagonal length: 30m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin) | era = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatori, Nara|ja|高取町|12=draft}}, Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == Overview == The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} were discovered during the excavation. The tomb is dated to the late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, making it a representative example of the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. == burial == The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure: * '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m. * '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm. * '''Construction:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section. * '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype. == Historical Context == The tomb is believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|ja|草壁皇子|12=draft}}, who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō. While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}} * {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} {{draft categories| [[ceb:Tsukamyojin Kofun]] [[ceb:Tsukamyojin Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Tsukamyojin revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 束明神古墳 |画像 = [[File:束明神古墳.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = 佐田束明神古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[高取町]]佐田742（字ヲヒラ）（春日神社境内） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 20.25 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 10.88 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[八角墳]] |規模 = 対角長30m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]]（内部に漆塗木棺か） |出土品 = 人骨・鉄釘・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]末 |被葬者 = （一説）[[草壁皇子]]（岡宮天皇） |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 八角墳 |ラベル = 束明神古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''束明神古墳'''（つかみょうじんこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[高取町]]佐田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[八角墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 [[草壁皇子]]（岡宮天皇、第40代[[天武天皇]][[皇太子]]）の真陵に比定する説が知られる。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]南縁、真弓丘陵南東部の尾根南斜面に築造された古墳である。[[1984年|1984]]-[[1985年]]（[[昭和]]59-60年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|束明神古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 墳丘は、斜面を大きく削り直径40メートルの平坦地を整地したうえで、[[版築]]によって構築される{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳形は八角形で、対角長約30メートルを測る{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南方向に開口する。凝灰岩の切石を積み上げた特殊な石槨で、平面形は長方形、断面形は家形（台形）を呈し、石槨内部には漆塗木棺が据えられたと見られる。石槨内の副葬品は失われているが、人骨片（歯）・鉄釘などが検出されており、石槨周辺では[[須恵器]]が出土している。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]末頃と推定される。被葬者は明らかでないが、第40代[[天武天皇]][[皇太子]]の[[草壁皇子]]（岡宮天皇、[[689年]]死去）の真陵とする説が知られる。当該時期の終末期古墳としては、大規模な地形改変の点、墳丘・横口式石槨の規模の点で際立った存在として注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 石槨は調査後に埋め戻され、現在では[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館の前庭で復元石槨が展示されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]末期、陵墓治定を忌避して墳丘・石槨の破壊。 * [[1893年]]（[[明治]]26年）の『大和国古墳墓取調書』に記載史跡説明板。。 * [[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）の『奈良県高市郡古墳誌』に記載。 * [[1978年]]（[[昭和]]53年）、墳丘測量調査（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]）。 * [[1984年|1984]]-[[1985年]]（昭和59-60年）、発掘調査（奈良県立橿原考古学研究所、[[1999年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tsukamyojin Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|横口式石槨（復元）開口部}}{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館展示。}}]] [[File:Tsukamyojin Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|横口式石槨（復元）内部}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。凝灰岩の切石を積み上げた特殊な石槨で、平面形は長方形で長さ3.06メートル・幅2.12メートル・推定高さ2.50メートルを測る{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 石材の切石は、50センチメートル×50センチメートル×30センチメートルの方形である{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。奥壁は垂直に積み上げるが、側壁は高さ1.27メートル（5段）まで垂直に積んだのち内側約60度に傾斜して積み（推定5段）、断面形は家形（台形）を呈する{{Sfn|束明神古墳（世界大百科）}}{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。床面にも方形切石を二重に敷き、床面のみ漆喰の塗布が認められる{{Sfn|束明神古墳（世界大百科）}}。天井部は破壊のため明らかでない{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨内では鉄釘多数（50本以上）・漆膜片・棺金具が検出されており、漆塗木棺の使用が推測されるほか、大型の釘の出土から木製棺台の存在が示唆される{{Sfn|束明神古墳（国史）}}{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 石槨内の副葬品は失われていたが、調査では人歯約10本（1体分：青年期から壮年期、性別不明）が検出されている{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また石槨周辺で[[須恵器]]が出土している{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 == 被葬者 == 束明神古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、'''[[草壁皇子]]'''（くさかべのみこ、岡宮天皇）に比定する説が知られる。草壁皇子は第40代[[天武天皇]]の第二皇子で、天武天皇10年（[[681年]]）に[[皇太子]]となったが、[[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]）に早世した人物である。妃の阿閉皇女（第43代[[元明天皇]]）のほか、子の[[文武天皇]]（第42代）・[[元正天皇]]（第44代）、孫の[[聖武天皇]]（第45代）、曾孫の[[孝謙天皇|孝謙・称徳天皇]]（第46・48代）は草壁皇子の血縁者であり、[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）に「岡宮御宇天皇」の尊号が奉られている。 草壁皇子の墓について、『[[日本書紀]]』に記述はない{{Sfn|真弓丘陵（国史）}}。しかし『[[万葉集]]』巻2の[[柿本人麻呂]]や舎人の挽歌によって、真弓岡への埋葬が知られる{{Sfn|真弓丘陵（国史）}}。また『[[続日本紀]]』では、[[天平勝宝]]7歳（[[755年]]）10月21日条に「山科、大内東西、安古、真弓、奈保山東西等山陵」に聖武天皇病気平癒祈願の遣使をしたとあるほか、[[天平神護]]元年（[[765年]]）10月15日条には称徳天皇が紀伊国に向かう途中で「檀山陵」で儀礼を行い、宇智郡に至ったと見える{{Sfn|真弓丘陵（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|束明神古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]では遠陵の「真弓丘陵」として記載され、[[大和国]][[高市郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北2町で陵戸6烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|真弓丘陵（国史）}}。 その後所伝を失ったが、幕末に束明神古墳の南にある現陵地の円墳（高取町森）に定められ、現在も[[宮内庁]]では「真弓丘陵（まゆみのおかのみささぎ）」として治定している。一方で束明神古墳に比定する説では、地元に草壁皇子の墓とする伝承が残るほか、大規模な地形改変の点、墳丘・横口式石槨の規模の点、文献との一致の点から有力視される{{Sfn|束明神古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館（橿原市畝傍町） - 束明神古墳の復元石槨を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（高取町教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=笹山晴生「草壁皇子」|ref={{Harvid|草壁皇子（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=戸原純一「真弓丘陵」（草壁皇子項目内）|ref={{Harvid|真弓丘陵（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=犬木努「束明神古墳」|ref={{Harvid|束明神古墳（国史）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|author=岸俊男|authorlink=川西宏幸|chapter=束明神古墳|year=|title=[[世界大百科事典]]|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|束明神古墳（世界大百科）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=真弓丘陵|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|真弓丘陵（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=束明神古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|束明神古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|authorlink=|year=1989|chapter=束明神古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=束明神古墳の研究 -奈良県高取町佐田-（橿原考古学研究所研究成果 第2冊）|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=束明神古墳の研究 -奈良県高取町佐田-（高取町文化財調査報告 第18冊）|publisher=高取町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} * [[草壁皇子]] {{デフォルトソート:つかみようしんこふん}} 石槨内の副葬品は失われていたが、調査では人歯約10本（1体分：青年期から壮年期、性別不明）が検出されている{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また石槨周辺で[[須恵器]]が出土している{{Sfn|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 == 被葬者 == 束明神古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、'''[[草壁皇子]]'''（くさかべのみこ、岡宮天皇）に比定する説が知られる。草壁皇子は第40代[[天武天皇]]の第二皇子で、天武天皇10年（[[681年]]）に[[皇太子]]となったが、[[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]）に早世した人物である。妃の阿閉皇女（第43代[[元明天皇]]）のほか、子の[[文武天皇]]（第42代）・[[元正天皇]]（第44代）、孫の[[聖武天皇]]（第45代）、曾孫の[[孝謙天皇|孝謙・称徳天皇]]（第46・48代）は草壁皇子の血縁者であり、[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）に「岡宮御宇天皇」の尊号が奉られている。 草壁皇子の墓について、『[[日本書紀]]』に記述はない{{Sfn|真弓丘陵（国史）}}。しかし『[[万葉集]]』巻2の[[柿本人麻呂]]や舎人の挽歌によって、真弓岡への埋葬が知られる{{Sfn|真弓丘陵（国史）}}。また『[[続日本紀]]』では、[[天平勝宝]]7歳（[[755年]]）10月21日条に「山科、大内東西、安古、真弓、奈保山東西等山陵」に聖武天皇病気平癒祈願の遣使をしたとあるほか、[[天平神護]]元年（[[765年]]）10月15日条には称徳天皇が紀伊国に向かう途中で「檀山陵」で儀礼を行い、宇智郡に至ったと見える{{Sfn|真弓丘陵（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|束明神古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]では遠陵の「真弓丘陵」として記載され、[[大和国]][[高市郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北2町で陵戸6烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|真弓丘陵（国史）}}。 その後所伝を失ったが、幕末に束明神古墳の南にある現陵地の円墳（高取町森）に定められ、現在も[[宮内庁]]では「真弓丘陵（まゆみのおかのみささぎ）」として治定している。一方で束明神古墳に比定する説では、地元に草壁皇子の墓とする伝承が残るほか、大規模な地形改変の点、墳丘・横口式石槨の規模の点、文献との一致の点から有力視される{{Sfn|束明神古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館（橿原市畝傍町） - 束明神古墳の復元石槨を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（高取町教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=笹山晴生「草壁皇子」|ref={{Harvid|草壁皇子（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=戸原純一「真弓丘陵」（草壁皇子項目内）|ref={{Harvid|真弓丘陵（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=犬木努「束明神古墳」|ref={{Harvid|束明神古墳（国史）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|author=岸俊男|authorlink=川西宏幸|chapter=束明神古墳|year=|title=[[世界大百科事典]]|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|束明神古墳（世界大百科）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=真弓丘陵|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|真弓丘陵（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=束明神古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|束明神古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|authorlink=|year=1989|chapter=束明神古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|束明神古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=束明神古墳の研究 -奈良県高取町佐田-（橿原考古学研究所研究成果 第2冊）|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=束明神古墳の研究 -奈良県高取町佐田-（高取町文化財調査報告 第18冊）|publisher=高取町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}} * [[草壁皇子]] {{デフォルトソート:つかみようしんこふん}} | designation1 = Not Historic Site1

'''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatori, Nara|Takatori, Nara]], Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== Overview ==
The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. 

The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* {{Cite book|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1999|title=Studies on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Nara Prefecture}}
* {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}}

== External Links ==
* {{Commonscat|Tsukamyojin Kofun}}

| burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin)

'''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatori, Nara|Takatori, Nara]], Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== Overview ==
The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. 

The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==

* {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}}

The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

'''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatori, Nara|Takatori, Nara]], Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== Overview ==
The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. 

The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

'''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatori, Nara|Takatori, Nara]], Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== Overview ==
The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. 

The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

| type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
| area = Diagonal length: 30m

| era = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]]
| designation1 = Not Historic Site1

'''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatori, Nara|Takatori, Nara]], Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== Overview ==
The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. 

The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

* {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}}

| coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 10.88
| type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
| area = Diagonal length: 30m
| burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin)
| era = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]]
| designation1 = Not Historic Site1
| architectural_styles = {{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}}

'''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatori, Nara|Takatori, Nara]], Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== Overview ==
The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. 

The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==

* {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

| image = File:束明神古墳 墳丘.jpg
| alternate_name = Sadatsukamyojin Kofun
| location = {{ill|Takatori|WD=Q1202681}}
| coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 20.25
| coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 10.88
| type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
| area = Diagonal length: 30m
| burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin)
| era = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]]
| designation1 = Not Historic Site1
| architectural_styles = {{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}}
}}

'''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatori, Nara|Takatori, Nara]], Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== Overview ==
The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. 

The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==

Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

'''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatori, Nara|Takatori, Nara]], Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== Overview ==
The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. 

The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

| type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

| burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (with a lacquered wooden coffin)

| designation1 = Not Historic Site1

'''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatori, Nara|Takatori, Nara]], Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== Overview ==
The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. 

The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==

* {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}}

'''Tsukamyojin Kofun''' (束明神古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatori, Nara|Takatori, Nara]], Sada, Nara Prefecture. The tomb is not Historic Site1 as a historic site but is believed to be the true burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== Overview ==
The tomb is located on the southern slope of the Mayumi Hills at the southern edge of the Nara Basin. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 1984 and 1985. 

The tumulus was constructed by leveling a large area of approximately 40 meters in diameter and building the mound using rammed-earth techniques. The tomb has an octagonal type with a diagonal length of approximately 30 meters. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber is constructed using tuff-cut stones, with a rectangular floor plan and a trapezoidal cross-section. The interior likely contained a lacquered wooden coffin. burial goods such as human bones (teeth), iron nails, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were discovered during the excavation.

The tomb is dated to the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]], making it a representative example of the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Based on its location, map_size, and construction, it is thought to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu.

== burial ==
The burial chamber features a unique horizontal stone structure:
* '''area:''' Length 3.06m; width 2.12m; estimated height 2.50m.
* '''Material:''' Rectangular tuff blocks measuring 50cm × 50cm × 30cm.
* '''built:''' The walls were built vertically up to a height of 1.27m, after which they were inclined inward at approximately 60 degrees, creating a trapezoidal cross-section.
* '''Interior:''' The floor was paved with two layers of stone, and a section of the floor was coated with plaster. 

burial goods recovered include more than 50 iron nails, lacquer fragments, and coffin fittings, suggesting the use of a lacquered wooden coffin. The presence of large nails implies that the coffin rested on a wooden plattype.

== Historical Context ==
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of [[:en:Prince Kusakabe|Prince Kusakabe]], who served as Crown Prince to Emperor Tenmu. Prince Kusakabe died prematurely in 689 AD, leaving a significant legacy through his descendants, including Emperor Monmu and Empress Genshō.

While the site is not officially Historic Site1 as a historic landmark, its map_size, construction, and historical significance make it an important archaeological site. The burial chamber has been reconstructed and is on display at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum.

== Related Facilities ==
* [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] Museum (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture): Reconstructed burial chamber from Tsukamyojin Kofun is on display.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==

* {{Cite book|editor=Takatori Education Committee|year=1999|title=Reports on Tsukamyojin Kofun|publisher=Takatori Town Education Committee}}
